title: Variables and Data Types
summary: Variables, primitive data types, type conversion, and constants in programming.
content_html:
Variables

A variable is a named storage location in memory that holds a value which can change during program execution.

// Java/C++
int age = 20;
double salary = 55000.50;
String name = "Alice";
boolean isActive = true;

# Python (dynamic typing)
age = 20
salary = 55000.50
name = "Alice"
is_active = True

Naming Rules

  • Must start with a letter or underscore.
  • Cannot use reserved keywords (if, while, for, etc.).
  • Case-sensitive: age ≠ Age.
  • Use descriptive names: studentAge not a (camelCase for Java/JS, snake_case for Python).

Primitive Data Types

TypeExamplesSize (Java)
int5, -3, 10032-bit
long3000000000L64-bit
double3.14, -2.564-bit float
float3.14f32-bit float
char'A', '3'16-bit
booleantrue, false1-bit

Type Conversion

// Implicit (widening) - automatic
int x = 5;
double y = x;   // int → double, safe

// Explicit (narrowing) - requires cast, may lose data
double d = 9.99;
int i = (int)d;  // i = 9, decimal truncated

// Python
x = int("42")    # string to int
y = str(42)      # int to string
z = float("3.14")

Constants

// Java: final keyword
final double PI = 3.14159;
final int MAX_SIZE = 100;

// Python: by convention (ALL_CAPS)
PI = 3.14159
MAX_SIZE = 100

String Operations

// Java
String s = "Hello";
int len = s.length();        // 5
String upper = s.toUpperCase(); // "HELLO"
String concat = s + " World";

# Python
s = "Hello"
len(s)          # 5
s.upper()       # "HELLO"
s + " World"    # "Hello World"
f"Age: {age}"   # f-string formatting
